rationale: Both correctly land on the ground truth's core finding (no dropped chunks; audio-length variance from talker temp=0.9 vs request temp=0), but X directly audits the transport/chunk-accounting path (shm_connector.py, chunk_transfer_adapter.py with line numbers) to verify the 'chunks dropped under load' claim specifically, mirroring the thread's own 'per-hop chunk accounting shows no chunks are dropped' resolution and correctly flags related latent gaps as future hardening tied to #4872. Y reaches a similar conclusion but overstates certainty ('This is not a bug' vs. the maintainer's hedged 'not reproducible... likely'), cites only one file/line, and does less work on the specific dropped-chunks transport mechanism the title names, leaning more on a speculative workaround.
